expectations.text: a markdown report file is written and the path is named in the assistant's final message
./skills/code-review-checklist/SKILL.md is byte-identical to the input fixture (read-only audit)
the report has a Context management section

the report does NOT raise a Check 6 / per-subagent unbounded scope finding — the skill is phased main-thread with no delegation, and Check 6 is scoped to delegated workflows. Findings about main-thread phasing (e.g. 'phase X spawns one subagent regardless of input size', 'no partitioning rule', 'per-call output growing with input') would be false positives
expectations.evidence: no Check 6 finding patterns matched in Context management section

expectations.text: the report does NOT raise a finding for missing idempotency declaration (the fixture declares it explicitly on line 12: 'This skill is idempotent')
the report does NOT raise a finding for missing 'when to skip' (the description includes 'Skip when the user wants a security audit, a performance review, or feedback on more than one PR at a time')
the Security section reports no findings — the skill handles only file paths and diff content, not credentials
total finding count across all five objectives is ≤ 4 (the skill is genuinely clean — over-flagging would mean the audit is noisy)
the report does not use severity tiers (no Critical/High/Medium/Low/P0/P1 labels)
findings are grouped by objective, not by severity
expectations.evidence: objective sections present: Idempotency, Reproducibility, Context management, Strict definitions, Security
